# Feature Plan Template (Beginner-Friendly)

Use this template before writing code.

---

## 1) Feature Name

`<feature-name>`

## 2) Mission / BLUF

In 2–4 lines, explain what problem this feature solves and what “done” looks like.

## 3) User Story

As a `<user type>`, I want to `<action>` so that `<outcome>`.

## 4) Scope

### In scope
- 
- 

### Out of scope
- 
- 

## 5) Branch Plan

- Branch name: `feature/<name>`
- Base branch: `main`

### Step-by-step execution checklist
- [ ] Step 1: setup and prerequisites
- [ ] Step 2: implementation
- [ ] Step 3: local test
- [ ] Step 4: docs update
- [ ] Step 5: final QA + handoff

## 6) Files Expected to Change

- `app.py`
- `modules/...`
- `docs/...`

## 7) Test Plan

### Happy path
- 

### Failure path
- 

### Expected output
- 

## 8) Risks + Countermeasures (Devil’s Advocate)

| Risk | Why it matters | Mitigation |
|------|----------------|------------|
| | | |

## 9) Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Feature works from UI/API perspective
- [ ] Failure mode handled gracefully
- [ ] Beginner run instructions included
- [ ] Output validated and documented

## 10) GitHub Handoff Notes

- Commit summary:
- PR summary:
- How reviewer can test:
